mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5%, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, depressing equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and residential housing starts indicates a broad-based demand shock that overrides monetary policy support, leading to negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 55% of out-of-sample instances, or the interaction term coefficient in a rolling regression becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation regime where CPI exceeds 300, further increases in the federal funds rate fail to anchor expectations and instead trigger equity risk premium compression, yielding negative forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: The negative return predictive power vanishes when CPIAUCSL falls below 300, or forward returns in the high-inflation regime show no statistically significant difference from baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal savings reflect stronger household balance sheets that sustain consumption expenditure, driving positive corporate earnings growth and equity returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising savings periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production falls below 110, it indicates severe supply chain bottlenecks or capacity constraints that trigger stagflationary pricing pressures, depressing equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where employment growth accelerates while real savings decline signals wage-driven consumption that outpaces income, eventually triggering inflationary feedback loops that force tighter monetary policy and negative equity returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 55% of out-of-sample instances, or the interaction term coefficient in a rolling regression becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid GDP growth, housing starts act as a leading indicator of credit demand; a decline in housing starts while GDP expands signals a credit crunch that reverses equity gains over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The negative return predictive power vanishes when GDPC1 growth falls below 2.0%, or forward returns in the specified regime show no statistically significant difference from baseline.
